_about: Where a pirated online course actually lives, and how to get it removed. First-hand routes and confirmation methods used by Delister (https://delister.co) on real filings. CC BY 4.0. Corrections and additions welcome via pull request.
_confirmation_note: Confirm a removal at the FILE, not the listing page. A listing can stay up while pointing at a file that is already dead. Some ISPs also intercept piracy domains and serve a block page with HTTP 200, so a raw request can read 'still live' when the site is unreachable or already down. When a host is on such a network, verify through a server-side text proxy (for example https://r.jina.ai/<url>) and resolve DNS over HTTPS rather than the local resolver.

file_hosts.name: MEGA
file_hosts.method: Email the designated copyright agent, or use the in-app report form. Name the exact file or folder link.
file_hosts.confirm: MEGA API: POST https://g.api.mega.co.nz/cs?n=<handle> with body [{"a":"f"}]. Returns -16 (EBLOCKED, taken down), -9 (ENOENT, deleted), or a JSON node (still live). The <handle> is the string after /folder/ or /file/ in the link, before any # key.
file_hosts.notes: Compliant mainstream host. Removing the file at MEGA kills every listing that links to it at once.

file_hosts.name: Pixeldrain
file_hosts.method: Email in English. Line one must say it is a copyright infringement notice (their classifier keys on that phrasing; 'stolen' or 'theft' is too vague). Put the pixeldrain file links in the body, complete and unobfuscated. No attachments.
file_hosts.confirm: GET https://pixeldrain.com/api/file/<id>/info -> availability field reads 'unavailable_for_legal_reasons' when taken down; the download endpoint https://pixeldrain.com/api/file/<id> returns HTTP 451.
file_hosts.typical_response: first report is reviewed by hand, then the sender address is whitelisted and later reports process automatically
file_hosts.notes: Netherlands operator with a published abuse process. Worth getting the first notice exactly right.

file_hosts.name: Send
file_hosts.method: Plain-text email with the seven DMCA elements (owner, agent authority, agent contact, work + original URL, infringing URLs, good-faith statement, penalty-of-perjury statement and signature).
file_hosts.confirm: Fetch the file URL: a removed file shows 'The file you were looking for' (does not exist).
file_hosts.notes: File host, not a listing site. Formerly send.cm, now branded send.now.

file_hosts.name: Podaon SIA
file_hosts.method: Email the provider's RIPE abuse contact. Podaon is an EU-established hosting provider and RIPE LIR (Riga, Latvia), so it carries DSA notice-and-action duties and a mandated abuse mailbox.
file_hosts.confirm: Fetch the file/listing URL through a server-side proxy (see _confirmation_note).
file_hosts.notes: Provider behind in-house lockers such as gfxdrive.com when the locker's own /dmca page is an empty stub. Identify the host via WHOIS/RIPE (IP -> netname -> org -> abuse contact).

listing_sites.name: design.rip
listing_sites.method: Use the site's own intake at https://design.rip/dmca (separate from any CDN relay).
listing_sites.notes: Listing site with a working direct takedown channel.

listing_sites.name: Psdly
listing_sites.method: Site's own 'DMCA - Urgent Content Remove' form, or email the admin address.
listing_sites.confirm: The listing page is gone or no longer serves the course.
listing_sites.notes: The .co.uk, .com, .io and .net domains 301-redirect between each other and are the same site.

search_and_cdn.name: Google Search removal
search_and_cdn.method: File a Search removal request naming the infringing URLs. De-indexing stops the page appearing in results, where nearly all the traffic comes from, even when the host ignores notices.
search_and_cdn.confirm: Google Removals Dashboard (reportcontent.google.com/dashboard), not a live search spot-check.
search_and_cdn.notes: A URL already carrying a settled counter-notice returns status 'Reinstated' and will not be removed again on a fresh notice; that requires court action, not a refiled form.

search_and_cdn.name: Cloudflare abuse
search_and_cdn.method: One submission per hostname; a human ticks the Turnstile checkbox.
search_and_cdn.confirm: Cloudflare emails an acknowledgment with a case ID; it forwards the complaint to the origin host and does not itself remove content.
search_and_cdn.notes: Lowest-yield layer: Cloudflare relays to the host rather than taking anything down. Go to the host directly where possible.

unactionable.name: Torrents / magnet links
unactionable.notes: A magnet or torrent has no host holding a file to notice; the content moves peer to peer. Notice-and-takedown does not apply. Be honest about this rather than leaving it looking unhandled.
